what_it_does: Defines an IIFE that attaches a libraryCovers array to a global DNA namespace, filling it with base64-encoded JPEG data URIs representing harvested design-library cover images.
observations: Comment claims the covers are real images harvested from vt-*/landing sources and are source-faithful
The array items are inline data:image/jpeg;base64 strings, making the file data-heavy
No imports, exports, or runtime logic beyond namespace initialization
Uses a global g.DNA = g.DNA || {} pattern to avoid clobbering an existing namespace
File content shown is truncated after many base64 entries
No code symbols are present; the file is purely an asset payload
standouts: Inline base64 image asset list under a global DNA namespace
Explicit provenance comment stating the covers are auto-harvested and not hand-edited
No executable behavior beyond initializing the array
conventions: IIFE wrapper for global namespace mutation
data:image/jpeg;base64 inline encoding for image assets
Metadata comment describing derivation tool (scratchpad/harvest.py) and editing policy
concerns: Large base64 payloads bloat the file and any bundle that includes it
Global DNA namespace could collide with other code
No visible checksums, versioning, or metadata fields for the images
Truncation prevents confirming the full array length or completeness
Inline binary data is difficult to review in version control
notes: A runtime asset module that exposes a global DNA.libraryCovers array of base64 JPEG covers, described as harvested from design-library landing pages.
questions: How many cover images are in the complete array?
What exact source paths or identifiers do the harvested covers correspond to?
Which component consumes DNA.libraryCovers and how are the images rendered?
Are there size or performance constraints for loading all covers at once?
What does the DNA namespace signify in this codebase?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches actual content: it is a derived, source-faithful library of cover image assets stored as inline base64 data.
apparent_themes: Global data registry for design substrates
intention_signals: To provide a portable, dependency-free set of real cover images that the design-library substrate can use at runtime without relying on external image files.
novelty: Packaging real design-library cover images as inline base64 data URIs inside a JavaScript runtime asset under a DNA namespace, with an explicit provenance and no-hand-edit policy.
contribution: Contributes a self-contained, portable image asset payload that a fused system could retain as a runtime cover library, independent of external file paths or network requests.
summary_for_embedding: library-covers.js is a runtime data asset that initializes the global DNA.libraryCovers array with base64-encoded JPEG data URIs representing harvested design-library cover images.
intention_for_embedding: Intended to supply source-faithful cover images to the design-library substrate at runtime without external image dependencies.
